_comment: Canonical NormalizedIssue shape per SPEC §4.1.7. Epic 2 E2-F smoke test asserts every ingested issue conforms to this contract. Field types are illustrative; adapters MAY add fields but MUST populate every required field on every issue they return from FetchCandidates.

external_url.format: absolute URL to the issue in the source tracker

description.notes: may be empty for tickets that carry only metadata

priority.notes: 0 = critical; nil when source tracker has no priority field

polaris_risk_id.notes: set for Orion-filed risk issues (§8.7) and tracker issues whose body links to a Polaris risk

orion_filed.notes: true when Orion created this issue via §8.7 auto-file

last_updated.notes: upstream tracker's last-modified timestamp; used by ingestion driver for `since` cursor

ingestion_invariants._comment: Asserted by E2-F live smoke. Each item is a shell-verifiable check the smoke script runs against the actual NormalizedIssue rows after ingest.
ingestion_invariants.external_id_uniqueness: SELECT count(*) FROM normalized_issue GROUP BY external_id HAVING count(*) > 1 → 0 rows
ingestion_invariants.no_empty_titles: SELECT count(*) FROM normalized_issue WHERE title = '' → 0
ingestion_invariants.state_in_enum: SELECT count(*) FROM normalized_issue WHERE state NOT IN ('open','in_progress','blocked','closed','cancelled') → 0
ingestion_invariants.external_url_absolute: SELECT count(*) FROM normalized_issue WHERE external_url NOT LIKE 'http%' → 0

eligibility_classes_observed._comment: E2-F asserts the live smoke against microservices-demo produces at least one issue in each of these classes (proves the eligibility evaluator works end-to-end).
